zh.Montgomery,Wales: 英國威爾斯蒙格莫利夏爾蒙哥馬利
zh.Montgomery, Wales: 英國威爾斯蒙格莫利夏爾蒙哥馬利
zh.lifeSummary: Mrs Gruffudd ap Grono ap Einion ap Seissyllt於大約1274年出生在英國威爾斯蒙格莫利夏爾蒙哥馬利。她於大約1297年與Gruffudd ap Grono ap Einion ap Seissyllt在英國威爾斯蒙格莫利夏爾蒙哥馬利結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Mrs Gruffudd ap Grono ap Einion ap Seissyllt于大约1274年出生在英国威尔斯蒙格莫利夏爾蒙哥馬利。她于大约1297年与Gruffudd ap Grono ap Einion ap Seissyllt在英国威尔斯蒙格莫利夏爾蒙哥馬利结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和1个女儿的父母。

nameMeanings.name: ap
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Hungarian Geza, Jeno, Arpad, Attila, Csaba.

Hungarian: variant of   Papp  , meaning ‘priest (of the Catholic or Orthodox Church)’. This surname is also found in Romania, Slovakia, Croatia, and Serbia, and also among Rusyns (in Serbia and Croatia). See also   Pop   2 and   Popa  .
English: variant of   Pape   1.

variants.name: Papp
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Hungarian Laszlo, Zoltan, Geza, Istvan, Gabor, Miklos, Tibor, Arpad, Denes, Imre, Attila.
German (Bavaria, Tyrol): from Middle High German pap ‘baby food’, Dutch pap ‘porridge, pap’, hence perhaps a nickname denoting someone who had lost his teeth or subsisted on a meager diet.

Hungarian: from pap ‘priest (of the Catholic or Orthodox Church)’, probably an occupational name for someone in the service of a priest or a nickname for a pious person. This surname is also found in Romania and Slovakia, and also among Rusyns. Compare Pap and Pop .

nameMeanings.name: Grono
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Fritz, Hans, Otto.
Perhaps an altered form of German   Grone  , or a habitational name from any of several places called Gronau.

variants.name: Grone
meanings: German: habitational name from Grone near Göttingen or other places so named in Lower Saxony.
North German (Gröne): variant of Grün (see Gruen ). Compare Groene .
North German: unflattering nickname for a lecher, from Middle Low German grone ‘lascivious’.

nameMeanings.name: Einion
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Irish (Tyrone): variant of   Moynihan  .

French: occupational name for a miner, from a diminutive of minier ‘miner’ (see Minier ). This surname is very rare in France; in North America it may thus (also) be an altered form of some other similar (like-sounding) French surname, such as Mignon .

variants.name: Moynihan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Patrick James, Donal, Eamon, Finbarr, Kieran, Niall.
Irish:

Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Muimhneacháin ‘descendant of Muimhneachán’, a diminutive of the byname Muimhneach ‘Munsterman’, i.e. someone belonging to the province of Munster. In Mayo this surname has sometimes been translated into English as Munster . There has been some confusion with Monaghan .

nameMeanings.name: Gruffudd
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Old Welsh Grip(p)iud, from an unexplained first element + a second element meaning ‘lord, prince’. Gruffydd ap Llewelyn ( d. 1063 ) was one of the most able rulers of Wales in the Middle Ages, scoring notable victories over the English until he was eventually defeated by King Harold in 1063 .

variants.name: Griffudd
meanings: From Old Welsh Grip(p)iud, from an unexplained first element + a second element meaning ‘lord, prince’. Gruffydd ap Llewelyn ( d. 1063 ) was one of the most able rulers of Wales in the Middle Ages, scoring notable victories over the English until he was eventually defeated by King Harold in 1063 .

variants.name: Gruffud
meanings: From Old Welsh Grip(p)iud, from an unexplained first element + a second element meaning ‘lord, prince’. Gruffydd ap Llewelyn ( d. 1063 ) was one of the most able rulers of Wales in the Middle Ages, scoring notable victories over the English until he was eventually defeated by King Harold in 1063 .

variants.name: Griffith
meanings: Anglicized form of   Gruffudd .

en.lifeSummary: Mrs Gruffudd ap Grono ap Einion ap Seissyllt was born about 1274, in Montgomery, Montgomeryshire, Wales, United Kingdom. She married Gruffudd ap Grono ap Einion ap Seissyllt about 1297, in Montgomery, Montgomeryshire, Wales, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter.

fr.lifeSummary: Mrs Gruffudd ap Grono ap Einion ap Seissyllt est née environ 1274, à Montgomery, Montgomeryshire, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni. Elle a épousé Gruffudd ap Grono ap Einion ap Seissyllt environ 1297, à Montgomery, Montgomeryshire, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille.

ja.lifeSummary: Mrs Gruffudd ap Grono ap Einion ap Seissylltは約1274年にイギリスウェールズモントゴメリシュアイアモントゴメリーで生まれた。約1297年にイギリスウェールズモントゴメリシュアイアモントゴメリーでGruffudd ap Grono ap Einion ap Seissylltと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。

ko.lifeSummary: Mrs Gruffudd ap Grono ap Einion ap Seissyllt(은)는 대략 1274년 영국 웨일스 몽고머이실르 몽고메리에서 태어났다. 그녀는 대략 1297년 영국 웨일스 몽고머이실르 몽고메리에서 Gruffudd ap Grono ap Einion ap Seissyllt(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다.

ru.lifeSummary: Mrs Gruffudd ap Grono ap Einion ap Seissyllt родилась около 1274 г. в местности монтгомири, монтгомиришир, Уэльс, Великобритания. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Gruffudd ap Grono ap Einion ap Seissyllt около 1297 г. в местности монтгомири, монтгомиришир, Уэльс, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь.
